page: Microsoft Azure DC8as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCACCV5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. This virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. However, it is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external persistent storage volumes as needed. It also does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng single-core evaluations. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics generally fall within the average range. Memory read bandwidth is average for both small and large blocks, but large block memory write bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Similarly, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is weak, while single-threaded compression and decompression tasks remain average.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the DC8as_cc_v5 offers a stable resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for web serving, database operations, and software compilation, where its average processing and memory capabilities align with standard operational demands. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff that lowers baseline resource overhead, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and do not require hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure DC8as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DCACCV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, running at 3.525 GHz, and 32.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, while multi-core processing, memory read bandwidth, and database operations score in the average tier. Conversely, large block memory write bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression are weak. This resource profile makes the server a balanced option for general-purpose workloads, such as web serving, software compilation, and standard database operations, where dedicated CPU allocation provides consistent performance without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC8as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM featuring an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 32 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC8as_cc_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated eight-vCPU virtual machine with balanced memory and strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads requiring external storage.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU, with support for Premium Storage
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
